question.text: An importer buys 1000 lots of USDINR futures at 84.00 to hedge USD 1 million payable. At payment, spot is 83.50. What is the effective cost and opportunity loss?

answer.text: Effective cost Rs. 84.00/USD, opportunity loss Rs. 0.50/USD (could have bought at 83.50 spot)
Effective cost Rs. 83.50/USD, no opportunity loss
Effective cost Rs. 84.50/USD, opportunity loss Rs. 1.00/USD
Effective cost Rs. 83.75/USD, opportunity loss Rs. 0.25/USD

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures P&L: 1000 x 1000 x (83.50-84.00) = -Rs. 500,000 loss. Spot purchase: 1,000,000 x 83.50 = Rs. 8,35,00,000. Total: Rs. 8,40,00,000. Rate = Rs. 84.00. Opportunity loss = 84.00 - 83.50 = Rs. 0.50/USD. **The Trap:** Futures lock in the rate — if spot moves favourably, the hedger cannot benefit. **Cross-Exam Context:** Opportunity cost of hedging is a key exam concept in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is why some hedgers prefer options over futures — options allow favourable rate participation.
